sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 12.25 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.12)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a finite group acting irreducibly on a vector space$V$. An orbit$^G$for$V$is said to be$p$-regu\-lar\/ if the stabilizer of$$in$G$is a$p'$-sub\-group. Does$G$have a regular orbit on$V$if it has a$p 0.1ex$-regu\-lar orbit for every prime$p$? { Jiping~Zhang} No, not always. For example, let$H={ A}_4 { Z}_5$, \$V=O_2(H)$, and$G$be a complement to$V$in$H$,$G$acting on$V$by conjugation. (V.\,I...
